name: SpikeInterface (Spike Sorting and Electrophysiology Analysis)

performance.notes: NumPy/SciPy-based; spike sorting is CPU-intensive; Dask for parallel

agent_readiness.notes: Unified spike sorting framework for extracellular electrophysiology. import spikeinterface as si. recording = si.read_intan('data.rhd'). recording = si.preprocessing.bandpass_filter(recording, freq_min=300, freq_max=3000). sorting = si.sorters.run_sorter('kilosort4', recording). we = si.create_sorting_analyzer(sorting, recording). we.compute('waveforms'). we.compute('templates'). we.compute('unit_locations'). Sorters: Kilosort, Mountainsort5, Tridesclous. Formats: Intan, SpikeGLX, Neuropixels, OpenEphys, NWB.

gotchas: Spike sorters (Kilosort4) require separate installation with complex dependencies
Requires neuroscience domain knowledge — not general-purpose
Heavy I/O — recordings can be hundreds of GB
Docker-based sorters needed for some algorithms on macOS
best_when: Neuropixels / SpikeGLX data preprocessing and spike sorting
Comparing multiple spike sorting algorithms on same data
summary: SpikeInterface unifies spike sorting and electrophysiology analysis. si.read_intan('data.rhd'). bandpass_filter. run_sorter('kilosort4', recording). create_sorting_analyzer. Compute waveforms, templates, unit locations. MIT. Supports Neuropixels, SpikeGLX, Intan, OpenEphys. Kilosort4, Mountainsort5, Tridesclous. Best for Neuropixels spike sorting pipelines. Sorters need separate install; Docker required for some on macOS.
